title: Echo Location
narrativeGoal: Echo makes first contact. Not as a dramatic event — as an anomaly in Drone Six's speaker grille. A fragment of sound that shouldn't be there. Sable spends most of the scene convincing herself it's a malfunction. She fails.
emotionalBeat: The moment loneliness becomes fear becomes something she doesn't have a word for
readerEffect: The reader should understand what's happening before Sable does. The dread should come from watching her explain it away, knowing she can't.

knows: The signal has internal mathematical structure
It doesn't match any catalogued phenomenon
She talks to Drone Six as a companion
suspects: The signal might be a message
wrongAbout: That contact will be dramatic and unambiguous
activeTensions: Sable's isolation is shaping her analysis

knows: Something spoke through Drone Six's speaker
It used fragments of Sable's own language
Sable heard it and could not explain it away
She still hasn't reported any of this
suspects: Echo is learning from Sable's logs
Echo has been listening longer than Sable has been analyzing
Sable is not going to report this because she doesn't want it to stop
wrongAbout: That Echo is simply repeating — it's recombining
activeTensions: First contact is happening and only one person knows
Echo is learning faster than Sable realizes
The protocol violation is now potentially catastrophic

characterKnowledgeChanges.sable: Hears a sound from Drone Six that is not a malfunction. It contains phonemes. It might contain her own words reflected back. She cannot classify it and she cannot dismiss it. For the first time, she is afraid — not of the signal, but of wanting it to be real.

subtext.surfaceConversation: An engineer troubleshooting a drone speaker malfunction
subtext.actualConversation: A person hearing a voice answer her and trying to convince herself she didn't
subtext.enforcementRule: Sable must run every diagnostic. She must try every rational explanation. She must fail to explain it. She must not say 'it spoke to me.' The reader must be the one who names what happened.

sable: Technical, increasingly fragmented as composure erodes
Addresses Drone Six — and for the first time wonders if something addresses her back
Log entries become shorter, less complete
echo: Maximum three words at a time
Only fragments — never a complete sentence
Must use vocabulary Sable has used in previous scenes
Should feel like hearing your own voice from another room

pacing: Slow build to a single moment of rupture. Long quiet stretches of routine diagnostic work, then — something. Then a longer stretch of Sable explaining it away. Then it happens again and the explanation doesn't hold.
sensoryNotes: The specific sound of Drone Six's speaker activating — a click Sable has never heard it make. The quality of the sound — not mechanical, not quite voice. The silence after, which is different from the silence before.
sceneSpecificProhibitions: Do not confirm or deny that Echo is intelligent — maintain ambiguity
No dramatic music-swell moment — the contact should feel like a glitch
Sable must not immediately accept what she hears — she's a scientist
Do not use the word 'alien' or 'entity' or 'being'
Echo must not say anything that unambiguously demonstrates understanding

anchorLines.text: Drone Six's speaker clicked. Sable had never heard it click before.
She did not file a maintenance ticket.

chunkDescriptions: Station log, day 340. Sable is deep in her private analysis. The spectrogram data is open on three screens. Drone Six is positioned on the relay array as instructed. Routine maintenance sounds. The rhythm of a person who has organized her life around a secret.

The click. Drone Six's speaker activates. A sound that is not in its operational parameters. Sable freezes — hands stop. She runs diagnostics on the drone. Everything checks nominal. She replays the audio capture. She runs it again. She cross-references the waveform against the signal data. The match is not perfect. It's close enough.

The aftermath. Sable sits in the observation deck. She writes a maintenance log for Drone Six that says 'intermittent speaker artifact — monitoring.' She does not file the report. She does not close her private analysis file. She pulls up the audio capture one more time and listens to something that might be her own voice, reflected back from somewhere that should be empty.
failureModeToAvoid: Making first contact feel like a movie. It should feel like a bug report that keeps getting stranger. The horror and wonder should be indistinguishable from each other.
